news.title: World Cup 2026: Spain training session cancelled before World Cup final
news.lede: Spain were unable to train on Saturday. Spain's final training session before the World Cup final was cancelled after heavy thunderstorms hit New York and New Jersey on Saturday. Luis de la Fuente's side were due to train at the Melanie Lane training ground in New Jersey but after the session was initially suspended, it was later called off. About five miles away in Morristown, opponents Argentina were later able to train after a 45-minute delay.

news.title: Shackle Rodri the metronome and Argentina’s passion play can floor Spain | Jonathan Wilson
news.lede: The World Cup final is a classic clash of the process team against a side riding an emotional urge to fulfil Messi’s destiny. In the 37 minutes between England going ahead against Argentina and falling behind, they had 12% possession. It’s fair to assume that in the World Cup final Spain will provide a rather different challenge. They do not suffer the Mafeking tendency of the English, seeking to re-enact some famous siege every time they take the lead.

news.title: Why Tuchel, Ancelotti And Super Coaches Struggled At The World Cup
news.lede: Top club coaches like Tuchel and Ancelotti surprisingly faltered at the World Cup, while international specialists De la Fuente and Scaloni led their teams to the final. The article suggests that club "super coaches," accustomed to extensive player contact and control, struggled to adapt to the limited preparation time and unique demands of international tournaments. Their inability to quickly build team chemistry and implement intricate tactics, unlike in club football, proved to be a significant hurdle. In contrast, coaches with experience within national federations demonstrated a better understanding of how to foster an ethos and tactical message efficiently, highlighting that international success requires specific adaptability beyond club achievements.

news.lede: Eye of the beholder, etc., etc. For the fútbol faithful, the World Cup excitement is building to a crescendo ahead of Sunday's final between defending champion Argentina and Spain. But for the football faithful? We've been subjected to plenty.

news.title: 🚨 Club World Cup: both finalists' training hit by weather ⛈️😱
news.lede: This World Cup weather, honestly... Argentina and Spain's preparations have been disrupted by their inability to train this Saturday. A thunderstorm led to the cancellation of both teams' training sessions, just over 24 hours before the World Cup final. The first to suffer were the Argentinians, who had to postpone their training session and go ride stationary bikes indoors.

news.title: Colombia and Argentina at the World Cup: Two Ways of Understanding Football and Life
news.lede: Colombia and Argentina have big differences. Before the tournament started, this World Cup gave Colombia the chance to vindicate the American dream of millions of Colombians who migrated to the United States searching for glory, yet the dream died early again. The group of players led by James Rodriguez, Luis Diaz, and Nestor Lorenzo promised they had prepared to stay as long as possible in the tournament. What was possible was five games.

news.title: Spain cancels training before World Cup final; Argentina delayed by storms
news.lede: Editor's note: Follow for live updates on England-France World Cup game. MORRISTOWN, NJ — Both teams competing in the FIFA World Cup final saw their final training sessions before Sunday's match affected by thunderstorms rolling through the northern New Jersey area where Spain and Argentina were set to practice. Spain was scheduled to begin its session at 11 a.m. ET, and its bus arrived as scheduled to the facility.

news.title: Where is the next World Cup 2030? Which countries are hosting?
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. The 2030 World Cup will be unlike any previous edition of football's biggest tournament. For the first time in the competition's history, matches will be staged across three continents, with six countries sharing hosting duties to celebrate the centenary of the World Cup.
